name: guilherme ferreira
bio: Software Engineering student at UMinho. Writing high-performance systems, building scalable architectures, and diving deep into low-level programming. Passionate about backend engineering, concurrency, and competent logic.
currently: reading the Tiger Book (Modern Compiler Implementation) and using Rust to implement the Tiger language
networking and learning from experienced backend and systems engineers
daily-driving and tweaking NixOS, with the goal of contributing back to the community

experience.title: Frontend Web Developer (Intern)
experience.desc: Architected an embeddable, high-density vendor dashboard for Shopify. Designed a lightweight data-sync layer using diff-based polling against backend microservices to minimize network payloads and ensure near-real-time data delivery.

experience.title: Co-Director (Social) & Tech Contributor
experience.desc: Co-directed operations for an 800+ student community. Core open-source contributor to CAOS and Pombo. Engineered the official ENEI platform for 550+ attendees, handling complex business logic and live interactions.

experience.title: Champion & CoderCamp Co-Organizer
experience.desc: Directing an association of 100+ mentors. Designed and executed a week-long technical summer camp for 30 participants, handling curriculum development, operations, and resource allocation.

projects.name: FlyData
projects.desc: High-performance in-memory database engine. Maximized throughput via SIMD, concurrency, branchless programming, and memory mapping. Built a cache-friendly architecture. Achieved 20/20.

projects.name: Pearl
projects.desc: Official ENEI event platform for 550+ attendees. Developed device-agnostic, adaptive interfaces focusing on high reliability under live-event conditions.

projects.name: LiveVue Core & Tooling
projects.desc: Contributed to the live_vue library and engineered a dedicated VS Code extension for sigil highlighting to improve developer experience.

projects.name: CeSIUM OSS Ecosystem
projects.desc: Maintained and contributed to open-source tools for UMinho students, including Pombo (Atlas & Astra) and the CAOS infrastructure.

goals: contribute to hardcore open-source (aiming for the Linux kernel or major backend tooling)
land a systems or backend engineering summer internship
master C & Elixir while diving deep into Rust and Go for performant backend logic
build high-throughput distributed systems and explore custom networking protocols
